senses.definition: a version of lacrosse played outdoors with ten players on each team

examples.x: He plays field lacrosse every weekend with his friends.
Field lacrosse games are played on large fields.
Field lacrosse has ten players on each team.
The field lacrosse team practiced passing drills.
He scored three goals in the field lacrosse match.

senses.definition: a version of lacrosse played indoors in an ice hockey rink with the ice removed

examples.x: Box lacrosse features smaller teams competing in an indoor setting.
Players wear helmets, pads, and gloves in box lacrosse for protection.
Box lacrosse is known for its fast-paced and physical style of play.
Box lacrosse requires quick reflexes and tight teamwork due to the confined space.
Box lacrosse allows for a different strategic approach compared to outdoor play.

senses.definition: a smaller-sided version of lacrosse played with six players per team

examples.x: Lacrosse sixes is a fast-paced variant of the game, perfect for smaller teams.
In lacrosse sixes, each team consists of three attackers, two defenders, and one goalie.
Players enjoy the quick transitions and increased scoring opportunities in lacrosse sixes.
The smaller field size in lacrosse sixes encourages players to develop tighter ball control and precision passing.
Playing lacrosse sixes can help improve players' agility, decision-making, and teamwork.

senses.definition: a non-contact version of lacrosse played with a softer ball and plastic sticks

examples.x: Schools often use intercrosse to introduce students to lacrosse.
In intercrosse, players use plastic sticks for safety.
The soft ball in intercrosse reduces the risk of injury.

senses.definition: (lacrosse) a play where a player blocks a defender to free up a teammate for a pass or shot

examples.x: He set a perfect pick to help his teammate get open for the shot.
Using a pick, the attacker dodged past the defender easily.
He was penalized for setting an illegal pick during the game.
A strategic pick can change the flow of the game in your favor.
Setting a good pick can disrupt the defense and open up scoring opportunities.

senses.definition: (lacrosse) a player's sudden and agile movement to evade defenders while carrying or receiving the ball

examples.x: He executed a quick dodge to slip past the defender.
His dodge was so swift, the defender couldn't keep up.
Their dodges are key to their offensive strategy.
She's been practicing her dodge tirelessly to become a top scorer.
Her dodge allowed her to break free from tight defense.

senses.definition: the technique used in lacrosse by a face-off specialist to quickly gain control of the ball after the referee's whistle

examples.x: He executes a perfect clamp to win possession off the face-off.
The midfielder practices his clamp technique tirelessly to dominate the center.
The coach emphasizes the importance of a strong clamp in winning possessions.
They work on their clamp drills to refine their skills before the game.
A successful clamp sets the tone for the rest of the match.

senses.definition: a pass made by a player to set up a teammate for a scoring opportunity in lacrosse

examples.x: He made a quick feed pass to his teammate who scored a goal.
She always looks for the open player to make a feed pass.
They practiced their feed passes to improve their offensive plays.
His feed pass found the cutter perfectly for an easy goal.
They worked on their accuracy to ensure their feed passes reached their targets.

senses.definition: the act of moving the ball from defense to offense to start an attack in lacrosse

examples.x: The defenseman initiated the breakout by passing to the midfielder.
We practiced our breakout strategy during training to improve our transition game.
The defensemen work on their passing skills to facilitate smooth breakouts.
The opposing team's aggressive press disrupted our breakout attempts.

senses.definition: (lacrosse) a quick change of direction by an offensive player to create space for receiving a pass

examples.x: He executed a perfect v-cut to shake off his defender and get open for the pass.
She practiced v-cuts to improve her ability to create scoring opportunities.
They used v-cuts to keep the defense guessing and open up passing lanes.
The coach emphasized the importance of timing when making v-cuts.
She struggled with her v-cuts at first but quickly improved with practice.

senses.definition: (lacrosse) a defensive player leaving their assigned opponent to cover another player who is potentially a threat to score

examples.x: The defenseman executed a perfect slide to help defend against the fast break.
Effective slides can disrupt the opposing team's offensive strategy.
Without a well-timed slide, the opposing team could easily score.
Coaches emphasize the importance of communication when executing a slide.
A coordinated slide can shut down even the most potent offensive plays.

senses.definition: a situation in lacrosse where a penalty is called, indicated by the referee throwing a flag onto the field

examples.x: When the referee throws a flag down, it means there's a penalty on the play.
Players should immediately stop when they see a flag down on the field.
A flag down can result in a man-up opportunity for the opposing team.
Coaches often remind players to stay disciplined and avoid causing a flag down situation.
Teams need to capitalize on flag down situations to gain an advantage.

senses.definition: the defensive strategy in lacrosse employed by the team that is penalized and playing with fewer players on the field

examples.x: With one player in the penalty box, the team had to switch to their man-down defense.
The coach emphasized the importance of practicing man-down defense during training sessions.
The team's strong man-down defense frustrated their opponents' offensive efforts.
The goalie made several crucial saves during their man-down defense.
It's essential for every player to understand their role in man-down defense situations.

senses.definition: the act of moving around a defender again after initially getting past them in lacrosse

examples.x: The attacker's quick re-dodge left the defender trailing behind
A successful re-dodge can disrupt the opponent's defensive formation.
She made a clever re-dodge move to shake off the defender and advance toward the goal.
The attacker's re-dodge caught the defense off guard, leading to an open shot.
She used a quick re-dodge to avoid the defender's stick check.

senses.definition: to rock the stick back and forth to keep the lacrosse ball secure in the pocket while running

examples.x: He cradles the ball tightly as he dodges the defender.
They are practicing how to cradle the ball smoothly while running.
He had cradled the ball perfectly until he got checked.
She cradled the ball to keep it from falling out during the sprint.
You need to cradle the ball to maintain control in the game.
